# Parallel Programming Homework (OpenMP)

This folder contains three tasks focusing on OpenMP parallelization techniques such as race conditions, critical sections, and reductions.

## Task 1: Parallel Histogram
Computes the frequency of values (0-255) in a large array using three different approaches:
* Naive parallel (contains race conditions)
* Critical sections (thread-safe but slow)
* OpenMP array reduction (efficient and safe)

**Example Output:**
```
Printing Naive { 364881 365063 365249 365927 361019 360928 361250 360949 360436 360846 360534 361607 360953 361093 361346 360581 360891 361849 360881 361509 360051 361692 361423 361900 361352 360983 361733 361791 361050 361280 360626 361683 361626 361138 360341 361154 361529 360464 360648 360721 361278 361406 361652 360688 361953 361808 362112 360671 361047 359687 360931 361036 362061 361481 360193 361048 360819 360738 361164 360773 361003 360635 361190 360633 361391 359841 361666 361431 360130 361217 360588 360505 360479 361454 359513 360684 359771 360981 361004 360404 361616 359902 361867 361059 360881 361461 361687 360543 360932 361507 360307 361539 361047 360873 360242 360476 360751 360500 361021 360866 360061 360485 361101 360218 359330 360859 360286 361245 361374 360558 360720 361002 360481 360325 360364 360389 361366 360911 360381 360603 359832 360647 361142 360366 360815 360285 360856 361226 361548 359716 362488 360587 360852 360968 360873 360504 360542 360369 360696 361625 361661 361878 360748 361035 361190 361814 361877 360093 361336 361249 361863 361758 360982 361221 361005 361785 361826 360834 362359 362040 360843 361133 360936 360660 361484 361533 360769 361419 361566 360292 361449 360659 360970 362142 361415 361047 361008 361179 360820 360677 360952 360955 360854 361024 360803 359914 361070 360287 360816 360943 361406 361040 360828 361544 360937 360837 361125 360612 360512 359999 361372 361110 359700 360483 361255 362469 360666 360539 359912 359822 361091 360666 361164 361083 359625 360795 360193 360722 360794 360254 361065 360345 360489 361029 360267 360089 360697 361090 360590 359971 359724 360674 360500 360769 360852 361052 360852 360390 360934 360230 359647 360495 359628 360199 361032 361203 360948 361600 360131 361880 361249 360931 362013 360473 360553 362100 }
```

> **_Note:_** Naive results will differ from Reduction results due to race conditions

---

## Task 2: Global Minimum Distance
Finds the smallest absolute difference between any two consecutive floating-point elements in an array of 50 million values using `reduction(min: ...)`.

**Example Output:**
```
Global Minimum Distance: 1.490116e-08
```

---

## Task 3: Parallel Filtered Sum
A two-step parallel process:
1. Finds the maximum value in an array.
2. Calculates the sum of all elements greater than 80% of that maximum.

**Example Output:**
```
Max: 1.000000, Threshold: 0.800000, Filtered Sum: 17989348.188802
```
